entry.title: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Selecting the right analytical tool within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud framework can be pivotal to addressing your business needs effectively. Embedded analytics within SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a suite of tools to answer diverse business questions, foster data-driven decision-making, and streamline operations. Here, we’ll delve into the factors that should guide your choice in selecting the most suitable embedded analytical tool for your organization and requirements. 

  1. Tailored Support for Diverse Needs
  2. Overview on all available analytical tools inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Review Booklet
    • Multidimensional Analysis
    • Overview Page
    • Analytical List Page
    • Smart Business KPIs
    • Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story
    • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel
  3. Checklist
  4. Feature / Function / Capability focus
  5. Self-service learning

Tailored Support for Diverse Needs

The landscape of business analytics is vast, and having the correct tool set can significantly enhance business operations by:

  • Addressing specific business queries and needs.
  • Increasing user adoption and engagement with analytical tools.
  • Enabling business self-service for ongoing data insights into operations.

When navigating through SAP S/4HANA Cloud's embedded analytics offerings, consider the following factors to align with your business goals: 

  1. 🧠 Key User Availability & Expertise: Ensure that the users have the necessary skills and expertise to interact with the chosen tool.
  2. 🏢 Line of Business (LoB): Matching the tool to the specific needs of departments such as finance, sales, or procurement can lead to more insightful analysis.
  3. 📅 Report Type and Cadence: Determine whether you require mass reports, individual reports, or a mix; and establish the frequency of these reports (daily, weekly, monthly).
  4. 📊 Report Complexity: Evaluate the complexity regarding multiple KPIs and dimensions to choose a suitable tool.
  5. 🌐 Business Domain Coverage: Establish whether your needs involve one or multiple domains.

Furthermore, different personas use analytics to drive specific aspects of their roles, leveraging different types and levels of data to meet their unique objectives and contribute to the overall success of the company. This table outlines the personas, their roles related to analytics, and the recommended tools to support those functions on high level.

 

PersonaRole Description and ExamplesRecommended Product
Executive LeadershipHigh-level strategic decision-making, financial performance tracking, and overall business health assessment.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Business Data Cloud 
Managers (e.g., Sales, Supply Chain)Sales performance tracking, pipeline management, identifying sales opportunities/inventory management, supplier performance, and logistics optimization.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial AnalystsBudget analysis, investment performance, managing the company's liquidity, funding, capital management, organize and control receivables and collections, etc.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial PlanningProvides overall financial planning and performance management insights.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud e.g. via Rapid FP&A for SAP S/4HANA 

Now, let's go deeper and let me provide you an overview on the analytical tools that SAP offers inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. More information can also be found here.

back to top

Review Booklet

As this is one of the new kids on the blog. I'd like to start with the Review Booklet. In essence, Review Booklets provide pre-configured, user-friendly business pages designed to address common and pertinent business inquiries for your day-to-day tasks right out of the box. They merge application logic and direction with built-in multidimensional analytics, enhanced further by advanced AI capabilities as they evolve. SAP ships already multiple Review Booklets with out-of-the-box content. We also know that our content never hits 100% of your requirements, thus it is possible to adjust the content via the Review Booklet Designer. An image or video says more that thousand words when it comes to data visualization, so please take a look at this example of a Review Booklet:

Key benefits of Review Booklet:

  1. Consolidated Reporting: Gather multiple reports into a single booklet format, streamlining access and enhancing information organization for more efficient review.
  2. Predefined Layouts for Business Queries: Utilize ready-made layouts tailored to address specific business questions, providing immediate insights without extensive customization.
  3. Guided Analysis with Multidimensional Insights: Experience guided and flexible analysis modes enriched by contextual actions and generic pivoting, seamlessly combining application logic with integrated multidimensional insights to support thorough exploration and understanding

back to top

Multidimensional Analysis

The second 'new' kid is the Multidimensional Analysis report. Multidimensional Analysis offers a flexible and interactive way to delve into complex datasets, enabling users to uncover insights more intuitively. It begins with an analytical query in its default view, serving as a starting point for exploration. From there, users can pivot and drill down into the data, adding dimensions, measures, and structures to rows and columns to tailor the analysis to specific needs. This approach is particularly beneficial for advanced users, facilitating deep data exploration and empowering them to discover trends and patterns that drive informed decision-making. For more info, check out this blog. Multidimensional Analysis will replace the reports based on the 'old' technology of Web Dynpro Grid in the future, so better get yourself familiar with it.

Key benefits of Multidimensional Analysis reports:

  1. Flexible and Interactive Exploration: Enjoy adaptability through interactive pivoting and drill-down capabilities, allowing users to dynamically manipulate data for deeper insights.
  2. Customizable Data Views: Begin with a default view of an analytical query and progressively enhance your exploration by adding various dimensions, measures, and structures to both rows and columns for tailored analysis.
  3. Advanced Data Exploration: Cater to advanced users seeking comprehensive understanding, offering powerful tools for extensive data investigation and analysis.

Figure: shows a Multidimensional Analysis on Income Statement and how to add a calculation on the fly.

back to top

Overview Page

An overview page is a unique SAP Fiori app designed to enhance user experience by offering a streamlined structure. Each overview page is composed of predefined cards of varying types displayed on a canvas beneath a filter bar. Adjustments made in the filter bar apply universally to all cards within the app, ensuring cohesiveness in data presentation. Users can interact with the cards by clicking on them, allowing navigation to additional apps such as reports, KPI details, or transactional applications.

Key benefits of Overview Page:

  1. KPI Cards for Immediate Insight: Deliver a quick and comprehensive snapshot of a domain by showcasing key performance measures, enabling users to grasp the overall picture swiftly.
  2. Aggregated Data Display: Present data in a consolidated format, streamlining information to enhance clarity and facilitate informed decision-making.
  3. Navigation Options: Enable deeper investigation through seamless navigation, positioning overview pages as an ideal entry point for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Procurement Overview Page for Purchaser.

back to top

Analytical List Page

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is an integral component of SAP Fiori designed to transform business key performance indicators into dynamic analytical dashboards. These dashboards incorporate KPIs, charts, tables, and visual filters to provide insightful data visualizations. Powered by SAP UI annotations, ALP offers the flexibility to explore data from various angles, allowing for in-depth analysis of root causes and supporting timely decision-making. 

Key Benefits of ALP:

  1. Insight to Action is Key: Empower business users to process their work lists in context with their analytical findings, driving effective outcomes.
  2. Focus on Critical Issues: Easily highlight and address the most pressing concerns, and take immediate action without having to switch screens.
  3. Prioritize Efficiency: Sort and filter work lists to ensure that the most critical issues are prioritized and dealt with promptly.

Figure: Supervise Collection Worklist for Accounts Receivable Manager.

back to top

Smart Business KPIs (including Smart Business Runtime aka generic drill-down)

The KPIs are presented on tile surfaces using either simple numerical values or mini-charts, organized through the SAP Fiori Launchpad. Business users have the flexibility to select which KPIs to display on their personalized Launchpads, choosing from catalogs assigned to their roles. For deeper analysis, users can easily click on a tile to access detailed drill-down information. The Smart Business runtime environment provides access to detailed configurations of evaluations, enabling users to drill down and analyze KPIs in detail. The runtime is enhanced with smart controls, such as smart filters and smart charts, to further streamline the analytical process.

Key benefits of Smart Business KPI reports:

  1. Visual and Color-Coded KPI Representation: Display KPIs the SAP Fiori launchpad with simple numerical values, utilizing colors to offer immediate visual cues and enhance comprehension of performance levels.
  2. Instant Performance Overview: Provide an at-a-glance view of business performance, enabling quick assessments and timely decision-making.

Figure: Analysis section on the Fiori Launchpad for Accounts Receivables Managers opening the Smart Business generic drill-down app Overdue Receivables.

back to top

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story

The SAP Analytics Cloud embedded version integrates seamlessly with the SAP Fiori UI of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the so called Stories offer thereby an additional tool to analyze business data. Stories are designed to transform raw data into insightful visualizations, empowering users to enhance business performance and make informed, data-driven decisions including seamless navigation to and from other S/4HANA Cloud apps. These stories reveal hidden insights in your data by allowing the addition of text, images, and generating dynamic visualizations such as charts and graphs. Of course, SAP offers a large set of out-of-the-box content which can serve as a quick start and can be customized according to the requirements. 

Key benefits of an embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story:

  1. Powerful Visualization and Feature-Rich Dashboards: Create dynamic and visually compelling analytical dashboards that enhance understanding and decision-making.
  2. Interactive Data Exploration: Easily explore data through linked analysis, page filters, chart filters, and input controls, providing a user-friendly experience for in-depth insights.
  3. Advanced Data Analysis: Utilize location analytics, perform calculations on the fly, and analyze multiple queries for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Purchasing Spend Dashboard for Purchasers.

back to top

SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel

There is also an option to analyze business data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel. However, this reporting tool is not embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, but can be opened via an app from SAP Fiori Launchpad. 

Figure: Opening SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel from Fiori Launchpad. 

back to top

Checklist

Now consolidating all the information above, I come to the following checklists that shall help preselecting possible tool candidates by ticking the checkboxes according to your needs:

back to top

Feature / Function / Capabilities focus

Now that I provided an holistic overview on all the available options to analyze your business data, I'd like to get into the details. Important to know is that the following tables show the strengths of each tool, there is no one fits all solution as different information requirements and perspectives require different capabilities. 

I will explain the one or the other capability to make sure that you understand these tables correctly. Please leave a comment if something is not clear.

Legend 

++

Yes, supported and additional features/functions available

+

Yes, supported but limited (explanation in brackets)

-

Not supported

 

Visualization Capabilities

 

 

Analytical List Page

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories*

Smart Business KPI

Multidimensional Analysis

Review Booklet

Table

++

++

++

++

++

Bar /Column Chart

++

++

++

++

++

Geo Map

-

++

-

-

-

Single Values / Numeric Point /

KPI tile

+ (max. 3 global KPIs)

++

++

-

-

Line Chart

++

++

++

++

++

Waterfall Chart

++

++

++

++

++

Donut / Pie Chart

++

++

++

++

++

Variance / Difference support

+ (if variance/difference is a measure itself it can be added as second measure)

++

+ (needs to be enabled in underlying data source)

+ (user can add a calculated measure)

+ (user can add a calculated measure)

Freestyle text

-

++

-

-

-

Image

-

++

-

-

-

  • Single Values/Numeric Point: Simple KPI, e.g. sales volume, number of returns
  • Variance /Difference support:  a measure’s difference between time periods, e.g. difference of sales volume year over year (total or %)

Business User Capabilities (at runtime)

 

 

Analytical List Page

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories*

Smart Business KPI

Multidimensional Analysis

Review Booklet

Sorting

++

++

++

++

++

Ranking

-

++

-

-

-

Global filter/ Page filter/ Variable prompts

++

++

++

++

++

Number format

+ (can be changed by changing the user preferences on FLP)

+ (only during design time)

-

++

++

Conditional formatting / Thresholds / Semantic coloring

+ (only during design time)

+ (only during design time)

+ (only during design time)

++

++

Show totals (i.e. table)

+ (limited)

++

+ (only during design time)

++

++

Swap Axis (Layout option)

-

++

-

++

++

Switch from Chart to Table view (and vice versa)

++

-

++

++

++

Table freeze (e.g. specific column / row)

+ (only column freeze is supported)

++

-

++

++

Insight-to-action workflow / contextual decision

++

+ (context based navigation)

+  (context based navigation)

+ (context based navigation)

+  (context based navigation)

Display ID and / or Description

+ (only during design time)

++

-

++

++

Undo (e.g. last action)

-

++

-

-

-

  • Ranking: filter by measure and select N top/bottom, e.g. top 5 customers by sales volume
  • Table freeze: freeze col and/or row for scrolling, defined by business user
  • Insight to action workflow: within one view analytical and transactional data, e.g. find critical tasks that need action and act upon these

Functional Capabilities

 

 

Analytical List Page

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories*

Smart Business KPI

Multidimensional Analysis

Review Booklet

Support of hierarchies/ Drill down

-

++

-

++

++

Zero suppression

-

++

-

++

++

Calculation on the fly (during runtime)

-

++

-

++

++

Export Excel / PDF

++

++

++

++

++

Commenting

-

+ (analytical specialist needs to enable this explicitly during design time)

-

-

+ (currently limited to specific pre-defined review booklets)

Leverage generative AI

-

-

-

-

+ (currently limited to specific pre-defined review booklets)

Multiple Pages

-

++

-

-

++

Styling (e.g. corporate branding)

-

++

-

-

-

Saving personalized views: Support of Bookmarks / Variant management

++

++

++

++

++

Share via E-Mail / Teams

++

-

++

++

++

  • Zero suppression: determine whether rows or columns that contain zeros as values are displayed or not displayed
  • Calculation on the fly: business or key user can define a calculation on top of the measures that the underlying data source provides, e.g. A-B, or more sophisticated like a restricted measure (e.g. sales volume of all customers who bought a specific material)
    • Attention: Best practice to add own calculations via the Custom Analytical Queries & Services app
  • Commenting: business user can add a comment on a specific value (i.e. cell-based)
  • Leverage generative AI: predefined, ready to use Business Insights. Analyze and summarize data set, calculate and explain context relevant financial KPIs (e.g. Cost Center Review Booklet)
  • Multiple Pages: not about views that can be changed but real pages that group different domains, views next to each other, e.g. like in a book with chapters

Data Capabilities

 

 

Analytical List Page

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories*

Smart Business KPI

Multidimensional Analysis

Review Booklet

Ad-hoc reporting / Pivoting

-

-

-

++

++

Data write back

++

-

-

-

-

Source of data

OData

InA

OData

InA

InA

Multiple sources (i.e. queries) in one app

+ (only KPI tags can come from different queries)

++

-

-

++

Key User Tool – create app via Manage KPI & Reports app

++

++

++

++

++

Out of the Box: SAP pre-delivered content available (i.e. app)

++

++

++

++

++

  • Ad-hoc reporting: non-periodic to answer ad-hoc questions, either in a pre-defined format or generated on demand, i.e. slice and dice / pivot table like
  • Data Write Back: if end user sees a record in the data that needs action, the user can navigate directly to the object page application and modify the record. E.g. ‘Supervise Collections Worklist‘ app: Assign or remove collection supervisors to/from collection items.

*for stories based on Optimized Story Experience

back to top

Self-service learning

Some links are already embedded into the text, in addition I'd like to list my favorite links.

  • Learning Journey - Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics 
  • SAP Help.com SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics 
  • Out of the box content:
    • Review Booklets
    • Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories
    • For all others - Fiori Apps Reference Library – Filter by Application Type = Analytical , Line of Business and more

A special 'Thank you' to all the amazing colleagues who contributed with their expertise!

back to top

entry.title: Re: Library view - price and item description
entry.content: Given all the review and attempts made, a case could be opened. Please, mention these details in the case to help with the understanding of the scenario.

entry.title: Re: How to send control recipe to CPI (Cloud Platform Integration)
entry.content: It's expecting type T for RFC because the documentation outlines that it calls an RFC in an external system for this integration... hence type T.

entry.title: Re: Number of attempts - Invalid
entry.content:

Case ID : 5000155096 

Regards,

Voyalpad Usman Khan

Meaning having the basic WalkeMe enabled in the instance and for the advanced things an integration with another tool.

Imran

entry.title: Re: Requirement to trigger automated email on production...
entry.content: Sorry, I forget to add the second link I had in mind: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/know-how-to-set-up-documented-emails-sent-automatically-to-business/ba-p/12841741

entry.title: How 'Relaxed Flow' flag in Manage Recipes/Routings app affects Order Release and SFC quantities
entry.content:
In this blog, I will show you in detail how 'Relaxed Flow' flag in Manage Recipes/Routings app affects Order Release process and the released SFCs quantities.

When producing materials with 'Lot Size' > 1:

Upon Order Release, 'X' Shop Floor Controls (SFCs) are created, where 'X' is calculated as the 'Quantity to Release' divided by the order header Material 'Lot Size'.

Once created, each SFC is assigned a quantity equivalent to the respective material Lot Size. In the example above, for a Lot Size of 20, when releasing 100 as 'Quantity to Release', 5 SFCs of 20 quantity each are generated.

Partial order releases and releasing quantities smaller than the Material Lot Size are also supported.

The release behavior and final SFC quantities released are highly dependent on the 'Relaxed Flow' flag set in the routing within the Manage Routings/Recipes app:

In the example above, with a Lot Size of 20, if you perform three individual/partial releases with the following quantities: 15, 15, and 40, the result would be as follows:

- If 'Relaxed Flow' is NO: A brand-new SFC is released every time, quantity is not accumulated from one release to another, from one SFC to the next one.

Where:

    • 1st release of 15, created SFC D100743 of 15.
    • 2nd release of 15, created SFC D100744 of 15.
    • 3rd release of 40, created two SFCs D100745 and D100746 of 20 (the lot size) each.

- If 'Relaxed Flow' is YES: Quantity is accumulated from one release to another, from one SFC to the next one until it reaches the lot size.

Where:

    • 1st release of 15, created SFC D1006008 of 15.
    • 2nd release of 15, updated SFC D1006008 quantity to 20 and created SFC D1006009 of 10.
    • 3rd release of 40, updated SFC D1006009 quantity to 20 and created SFCs D1006010 of 20 and D1006011 of 10 remaining quantity.

You can learn more about 'Lot Size' > 1 and 'Relaxed Flow' at:

  • Handling SFCs with Lot Size > 1 in SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • Relaxed Flow Routings within SAP Digital Manufacturing

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Synchronise Tasks from Jira to SAP CALM
entry.content:
Hallo Team, 

  • I did integrated Jira with CALM, whereby when i create a task in SAP CALM, this will be created in Jira and also updated there, i did follow this instruction and did some Jira Automation to achieve that --> so when i do some changes in CALM, these will be adapted to Jira.
  • Now i want to do the oppposite, so when tasks are created, updated in Jira, these actions should also be adapted to SAP CALM
    • How can i achieve that, is there is an API from Jira to CALM
    • is there is any clear instruction, Guide How to?

Best wishes

Quteiba

entry.title: Re: How to send control recipe to CPI (Cloud Platform Integration)
entry.content: Yes we understand this, but our CPI can not receive this type of communication, so I am asking if there is a workaround either in SAP PP-PI or CPI.

Hi @Andressajulio3 

To help us to understand your query and to ensure that everyone in the SAP Learning Group can follow we kindly ask you to communicate in English
Kindly request to please reword your post accordingly.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Run Foreign Trade Regulations Z4
entry.content: removing tags SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Research and Development, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Master Data, as not relevant to this area

entry.title: How to determine the Feature a transport request is assigned to in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We have several Transports that were created without a CALM Feature Request which were later assigned to a Feature.  

Now we need to find out, which feature was assigned. Unfortunately the Transport Request Attribute CALM_CDM_FEATURE_ID doesn't really link to the feature. We can't search neither for the Transport nor this CALM_CDM_FEATURE_ID within CALM.

How can we find out, which feature a specific transport was assigned to?

Kind regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to determine the Feature a transport request is a...
entry.content:
The Transport Analysis application allows the user to search with the Transport Request ID and returns any matching entry which shows a wealth of information about the transport.

To access this application:

  1. Launch SAP Cloud ALM
  2. Click Implementation > Analytics and Reports > Cross-Project Overview
  3. Click Transport Analysis
  4. In Search, enter the Transport Request ID and press enter
  5. The results will return the transport, its title, status, the linked Feature, and the target tenant

entry.title: Re: 'initiate onboarding' button not visible
entry.content:
Hi @Mmeso94 

I have checked the feature permission on the Job Req template and RBP's 

Furhter, not having setup the Onboarding Integration in "Setup Onboarding Integration" can cause issues like this as well. 

Best
Jacob 🙂

entry.title: Re: Suspend and Resume
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing your tips! Please note that

  • OData V4 has a dedicated topic with regards to "Suspend and Resume".
  • v4.ODataListBinding#suspend specifies additionally in which cases an actual Error will be thrown.
  • Subclasses of TreeBinding do not support suspended. Cf. OpenUI5 issue #3161. If it's really needed in your project, please create a new OpenUI5 issue.

entry.title: Re: Consuming External API’s in ABAP Code Snippets
entry.content:
Hi @k_sood ,

Thank you for sharing detailed blog. With your code I am able to generate access token of External API.

But the challenge is ,in the code shared by you Client secret is hardcoded. Instead of hardcoding is it possible to use Oauth Profile as our client secret gets updated once in every 6 months.

Thanks

Deepthi

entry.title: Re: Possibility to edit default continuous feedback quest...
entry.content: Thank you! Is there any possibility to limit the number of questions or to hide the feedback topic field for instance?

entry.title: Re: Activating Unified Data Model for CSB - a complete beginners guide
entry.content:
Hi, 

thank you this was helpful. 

Once UDM is enabled, the options in Career Site Builder will change slightly. We will cover this in more detail in our next blog post.  
--> is the next blog ready yet?

entry.title: Re: Process Chain: load step success if file not found in AL11
entry.content: If the source file is not available, then the dtp/infopackage should fail. If you want the chain to continue despite the error, set the start condition of the succeeding steps in the chain to "Always".

entry.title: Re: Single Sign On - all Hybrid SAP landscape - One Identity
entry.content:
Hello Vasu;

For the Web based access on the on-premise systems you shouldn't have any major complexity. As the Web based uses HTTP protocol, it is compatible with SAML2 so you can centralize both your cloud solutions and on-premise(web based access) in IAS as proxy towards your Entra ID.

For the SAPGUI based access it gets a bit trickier but is also feasible. Different from the Web based which uses HTTP, SAPGUI access uses the SNC protocol which only supports Kerberos or X.509 certificates for SSO. In this case to centralize the SSO, you will need to use the Secure Login Service for SAPGUI, which is the replacement for SSO 3.0. This BTP service generates short-lived X.509 certificates so users can use it as the SSO token for SAPGUI. The BTP service supports SSO through IAS as proxy towards your Entra ID.

It works in a way that every user will need to authenticate against your corporate IDP to generate the certificates that will be valid between 1-12 hours(you can setup this in SLS for SAPGUI) if I'm not mistaken.

-For the cloud solutions you will need to refer to their product documents to see how it is implemented, but a lot of solutions already have wizards to execute the trust configuration towards IAS.
-For the Web based access on on-premise solutions (both Java and ABAP) this wiki should provide you some guidance: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/security/3362974627.html
-For the SAPGUI SSO with SLS for SAPGUI please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/security/3362974627.html

Please refer to the reference architecture on Cloud leading authentication as well: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-cloud-leading-authentication

Cheers!

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Antwerp
entry.content:
🚀 Calling all tech enthusiasts in the SAP world! 🚀 Join us for an evening of networking, mingling, and tech talk at our casual after-work event! 🌟 Whether you're a programmer, an SAP expert, or simply tech-curious, this is your chance to connect with like-minded SAP professionals in a relaxed setting. 🎉

📅Date: 15/05/2025

🕔Time: 18:00

📍Location: nobuco BRITSELEI 94 bus 31 3 D3 Antwerp

📅Agenda: 

18u welcome
18u30: Session on BTP by @WouterLemaire (45min)
19u15: Drinks + bites and talk

Get ready for an evening filled with stimulating conversations, and the opportunity to expand your SAP network. 💬 Bring your ideas, questions, and business cards – you never know who you might meet! 

Don't miss out on the chance to unwind, connect, and discover new opportunities in the SAP world. RSVP now to secure your spot! 📥 Space is limited, so act fast!

entry.title: PR Integration via WebService in CIG configured site
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know if we can use Web Service integration together with CIG integration method.

I need to use Web Service integration to load Requisition from third party system.

For standard Ariba-ERP integration we use CIG now.

If that's not possible, would be great to know what are the options.

We would like to avoid replication of PR to S4 and then to Ariba.

#managedgateway #CIG #webservice

entry.title: Re: Help with Printing at F110
entry.content: how are you printing now? payment advice? payment list?

entry.title: Re: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content: I don't see something generic. You say "for a user", do you mean you're interested only by dialog workitems? If yes, I guess you could enhance the end of the method CREATE_VIA_API or CREATE_VIA_WFM of CL_SWF_RUN_WIM_DIALOG. If no, I guess you could look at the method EXECUTE_INTERNAL of CL_SWF_RUN_CREATE_API_REQUEST or CL_SWF_RUN_CREATE_WFM_REQUEST which create the workitems.

entry.title: Re: Configuring OAuth 2.0 and Creating an ABAP Program That Uses OAuth 2.0 Client API
entry.content:
Hi @lpodile ,

Thank you for sharing detailed Blog. We have similar requirement. I have used the code shared in the Blog but getting status code 403.

Using postman also if we choose operation "Post" and hit Send Button we are getting similar issue. We have to choose Generate Token to get access token.

Is there any possibility to get access token in this scenario.

Thanks

Deepthi

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) for SAP Basis Professionals
entry.content:
This Blog is mainly written for the SAP Basis colleagues, Project Managers and other SAP consultants who are not much familiar with IBP. This blog will also provide some basis knowledge on supply chain planning operations.

SAP IBP is a next-generation supply chain planning tool hosted on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It combines:

  • Predictive analytics powered by AI and machine learning
  • Cloud-based architecture for scalability
  • Real-time collaboration across supply chain teams
  • Seamless integration with SAP ERP and S/4HANA

 

Why is SAP IBP Gaining Popularity?

  1. Cloud Flexibility – No on-premise infrastructure to maintain.
  2. Advanced AI Forecasting – Improves demand planning accuracy.
  3. Integration with SAP S/4HANA – Enables real-time data exchange.
  4. End-to-End Visibility – Optimizes global supply chains efficiently.

 

  •  Most of the companies are now working to move from SCM system to cloud based IBP system.

 

There are multiple other cloud solution for supply chain planning and one of the most popular tool is Kinaxis RapidResponse.

 

Now lets start with How SAP Basis Professionals Work with IBP

 

Like All other SAP cloud applications, SAP Basis team will be working on the technical configuration, integration of IBP with on-premise and other cloud bases applications.

 

SAP Basis teams play a vital role in IBP system architecture and administration:

 

Below is the diagram we have taken from SAP IBP documentation guide which explains different connections we can have with SAP IBP.

SAP IBP technical diagram Basis

IBP is used mainly for time series based integration (long term planning) and Order based integrations (short term). Based on the long term or short term planning integration type is selected.

Lets understand the integration types

  1. Time-Series-Based Integration

Time-series planning organizes data into period-based key figures (e.g., weekly, monthly, yearly) and is used for forecasting, demand planning, and inventory optimization.

 

Most customers used Time series planning as it is used mainly for long term planning or to handle demand planning related scenarios

 

Timeseries based integration

You can refer blog for integration of CI-DS with ECC.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/sap-erp-and-sap-ibp-technical-integration-using-ci-ds/ba-p/13579693

Below is the example of how IBP system will collect the data and perform the demand planning operations based on the historical data to create the forecasts.

Planning

Most of the Basis people are not aware of Planned Independent Requirement. PIR is used in SAP Demand Management to define anticipated demand for a product without being directly linked to sales orders. PIRs help businesses plan production based on forecasts rather than customer orders.

      2. Order Based Planning

Order-based planning focuses on real-time transactional data (e.g., sales orders, purchase orders, stock transfers) and is used for short-term execution and response planning.

Order based planning

Order based Planning connect ECC or S4HANA systems with IBP via cloud connector configurations.

 

With these two integrations types, Basis team can choose the different components they need to setup. 

If you have some experience in SAP Manufacturing processes you can refer some if the below IBP use cases

  1. Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP)

Use Case: A manufacturing company aligns demand forecasts with production capacity to avoid stock shortages.

  1. Demand Planning

Use Case: A retail chain predicts seasonal demand fluctuations using AI-driven forecasting.

  1. Inventory Optimization

Use Case: A pharmaceutical company minimizes excess stock while ensuring critical medicines are available.

  1. Supply Planning

Use Case: An automotive supplier optimizes raw material procurement based on production schedules.

  1. Response & Supply Execution

Use Case: A logistics firm adjusts supply chain routes dynamically based on real-time disruptions.

I believe if you have not started working on IBP yet, some of the above terms explained above will help you to get started.

entry.title: Re: Activating Unified Data Model for CSB - a complete beginners guide
entry.content: Hi Bianca. not yet, but working on it. will link the article once it's done.

entry.title: Kickstart Your Developer Journey – Get SAP-Certified in Low-Code/No-Code
entry.content:
New to development? No problem! SAP offers a certification and learning resources that help you build real business apps, workflows, and user experiences — without needing to be a coding expert. Using tools like SAP Build Apps, Work Zone, and Process Automation, you’ll be surprised how much you can do just by dragging, dropping, and configuring.

 

Simple Steps to Get Certified

 

Step 1: Learn at Your Own Pace Follow these beginner-friendly learning paths:

  • SAP Build Apps: Build apps visually without needing to write code. Learn how to connect data, create user-friendly pages, and define app behavior.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Set up a digital workspace where users can access their apps and content in one place.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to build simple workflows and automation tools to save time and reduce manual work.

Step 2: Practice What You Learn You’ll try a real-world simulation using everything you’ve learned. This hands-on practice is required before taking the certification exam and helps boost your confidence.

Step 3: Take the Certification Exam Once you feel ready, book your exam. Passing it proves your skills and gives you an official SAP Certification — a great addition to your resume!

 

Want to Keep Growing?

 

  • Watch tutorials on YouTube from SAP Developer Advocates.
  • Join local SAP CodeJam events to meet and learn from other developers.
  • Subscribe to SAP Learning Hub for more guided learning from SAP experts.

 

Whether you're starting your career or switching to tech, this certification is a great way to get hands-on with real business tools — and build your future.

entry.title: Re: Help with Printing at F110
entry.content: Hi all,

entry.title: Re: Help with Printing at F110
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Ariba Requisition Replication to S4
entry.content:
Hello,

I have requirement to create two separate processes in Ariba for the Purchase Requisition Creation.

We use CIG as integration method at the moment and only first point is configured.

1. Standard PR replication with Budget Check interface then PR approval in Ariba and PO generated from Ariba.

2. PR replication to S4 and further processing in S4 like approvals and PO.

Any advise how to configure will be appreciated.

#CIG #managedgateway

entry.title: Re: How to throw errors from REST API calls (CAP CDS and...
entry.content:
Hi T1mey,
Thank you for your reply. I use Kotlin for Handlers and I use the req.err equivalent which is ServiceException. I use it as below

catch (e: RestClientResponseException) {
            log.error { "Failed. Response: ${e.statusCode} ${e.responseBodyAsString}" }
            if (e.statusCode == NOT_FOUND) {
                messages.errorCode("notFound")
            }
            messages.errorCode("error", e.responseBodyAsString)
            messages.throwIfError()
        }

entry.title: Re: Help with Printing at F110

entry.title: Re: Table(s) containing Views and their table(s) information
entry.content: View definitions DD25L Stores view header definitions (basic info about views). View to base tables DD26S Defines base tables used in a view and their relationships (join conditions). View field mappings DD27S Field mapping from base tables to view fields. View selection conditions DD28S Selection conditions used in views (like WHERE clauses).

entry.title: Re: Actions in Behavior Definition For CDS Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hello,

In the SetExchange(for Action) method, the Keys values are coming through successfully.

What I want to achieve is to update the icons on the screen based on these key values and refresh the UI accordingly.

Additionally, after calling the SetExchange( is empty code ) method, the if_rap_query_provider~select method is triggered.

In this call, the low value from io_request->get_filter()->get_as_ranges() is empty.

I couldn't find a clear example addressing this specific scenario.

Kind regards, aatan.

entry.title: Re: 📅 Day 4/30 – Understanding the Bill of Materials (BOM) in SAP PP📘🔩🛠️
entry.content:
Alternative BOMs are useful in:

  • Managing material shortages
  • Supporting product variations
  • Optimizing cost or performance

 

You select the appropriate BOM version based on production strategy, availability, or customer requirements.

entry.title: Implementation of German Electronic Invoices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

2. Is the DRC cloud edition solution required to implement German electronic invoices?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Implementation of German Electronic Invoices in SAP S...
entry.content:
Yes, you can implement German electronic invoice solution with only a Dutch company code and DRC cloud edition solution is required to implement German electronic invoices.

For more details please refer to the below KBA - 3604801

entry.title: Excel based Fiori App development
entry.content:
We have a redesign a web app in Fiori which is currently exist in Mainframe and fetching the data from Oracle DataMart, its key functionality includes:

1- Excel based interface, which allow users to check-in/out to edit any field (includes close to 300 fields).
2- All users can create a variation of the material master view(may be 200-300)

and client is on GCP as Iaas, so what are the possible options , based on our analysis we have following options

Option 1: SAP Fiori + CAP
Frontend Framework SAP UI5 (Fiori Smart Table)
Business Logic Layer SAP CAP (Node.js) on BTP using JavaScript or TypeScript for excel check-in/out

Option 2: React + AG Grid on GCP
Frontend Framework React with AG Grid / Handsontable
Business Logic Layer Custom Node.js APIs on GCP


Option 3: UI5 + AG Grid (Hybrid)
Frontend Framework:SAP UI5 Shell + Embedded AG Grid/Handsontable
Business Logic Layer SAP CAP (Node.js) on BTP using JavaScript or TypeScript for excel check-in/out

We are thinking for Option-3 or Option-2 as Smart-Table is not as flexible as AG/Handsontable, what's your thought, please provide your recommendations

entry.title: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP: Help embracing Clean Core Principles and approach
entry.content:
The transition of customers to SAP S/4HANA represents more than a technical upgrade —it’s a transformation in how enterprise systems are extended, integrated, and future-proofed. At the heart of this shift is the Clean Core principle: minimizing modifications to the core ERP to maximize agility, maintainability, and upgradeability.

Clean Core is not just a best practice—it’s a technical debt reduction strategy that future-proofs the ERP landscape while promoting agility and resilience and is crucial for a fast adoption of AI based innovations.

As organizations adopt SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to build side-by-side extensions, PostgreSQL on SAP BTP can be a suitable option in addition to SAP HANA or when it does not make sense to use the full HANA capabilities, for storing and managing data outside the core system. 

Here’s why PostgreSQL on SAP BTP is not just a database option—but an important enabler for clean core success. 

What is the Clean Core approach?

The Clean Core principle emphasizes a decoupled, modular architecture where:

  • Custom logic should whenever possible built outside the SAP S/4HANA core.
  • Core system upgrades are simplified due to reduced custom code.
  • Extensions can leverage modern technologies via SAP BTP.

A decoupled model leans heavily on side-by-side extensibility: offloading custom applications, workflows, and data persistence to SAP BTP, while keeping the digital core stable and uncluttered.

PostgreSQL on SAP BTP: A 'natural' fit

PostgreSQL is an open-source, fully managed relational database available on SAP BTP, offering the reliability of enterprise SQL combined with the flexibility demanded by cloud-native applications. Here’s why it’s relevant in a Clean Core strategy:

1. Decoupled persistence for custom applications

With PostgreSQL, custom applications hosted on SAP BTP can maintain their own data persistence layer, avoiding unnecessary entanglement with the ERP core. This means:

  • Instead of creating all custom tables within S/4HANA, developers can now manage many of them externally.
  • Upgrade paths for the core ERP experience minimal disruption with less impact.

Developers have greater control over data structures and access patterns.

2. Open standards and developer productivity

PostgreSQL’s SQL compliance and broad integration support accelerate development.

  • Use standard APIs together with CAP CDS for rapid prototyping and developing  
  • Integrate with CAP (Cloud Application Programming model) CDS Postgres database service
  • Available on Kyma (Kubernetes-based runtime) on SAP BTP designed for building and running cloud-native microservices and serverless functions.

3. Cost-effective and scalable

As a managed offering, PostgreSQL on SAP BTP delivers:

  • Lower total cost of ownership compared to other database options.
  • Easily scalable to handle increasing workloads.
  • High availability and backup support out-of-the-box.
  • Available to be consumed via CPEA/BTPEA or as Pay-as-you-go.
  • Free-Tier option available to support prototyping or development/testing scenarios.

4. Data governance and security

PostgreSQL on SAP BTP supports enterprise-grade compliance:

  • Option of using only BTP private end-points for a more secure data access. 
  • Use the encryption capabilities provided by the underlying IaaS providers
  • SSL is enabled by default and is enforced.

Clean Core in action: A Side-by-Side extension example

Scenario: Retail Chain Promotion Approval Workflow

Traditional (Non-Clean Core) approach: 

A retail company wants to manage promotional discounts across stores with their own (non-standard) workflows and specific business requirements. In a non-clean core setup, custom logic for promotion creation, approval workflows, and validations (e.g., margin checks) will need to be added directly inside SAP S/4HANA, involving custom tables, workflows, and most likely UI changes.

Clean Core (Side-by-Side) approach: 

With Clean Core principles, the retailer can build a promotion management app on SAP BTP, for example, using:

  • CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) for service logic
  • PostgreSQL to store promotion drafts, approval history, metadata, and other relevant data
  • SAP Fiori or SAP Build Apps for a user-friendly interface

Integration with S/4HANA via APIs to:

  • Retrieve product and pricing data
  • Push approved promotions to relevant systems (e.g., Pricing system, POS system, ...)

To sum up

  • ☑️ S/4HANA remains clean — no custom tables or code inside the core
  • ☑️ Retail process is decoupled — easy to evolve without touching ERP
  • ☑️ PostgreSQL stores request and approval data — independently

 

Further readings:
  • Practicing Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Learning)
  • SAP’s official learning journey explaining the Clean Core concept and its implementation.
  • SAP BTP PostgreSQL Documentation
  • Technical docs for configuring, deploying, and managing PostgreSQL within the SAP BTP environment.
  • Core Data Services (CDS) in CAP
  • Understand how data modeling in SAP Cloud Application Programming Model compares to traditional ORM approaches.
  • SAP Kyma Runtime Overview
  • Explore how to build and run microservices and event-driven applications in a Kubernetes-native environment.
  • PostgreSQL Official Documentation
  • Comprehensive guide to PostgreSQL features and SQL best practices.

 

Thanks for reading!
For more insights from the product team on PostgreSQL and Redis services on SAP BTP, explore:
  • PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - Are you looking for official blog posts? Blog INDEX
  • Redis on SAP BTP - INDEX of official blog posts
Regards, 
Antonio

entry.title: Error uploading content module: HTTP server: Read timed out
entry.content:
Hi,

I am developing a React application to deploy on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment . The initial deployments went well and the application was correctly accessible from HTML5 Applications. Now I can't deploy anymore, I get the following err
"Error uploading content module "PMShift_ui_deployer" in service "PMShift_html_repo_host": Error occurred during communication with HTTP server: Read timed out"
Now the content zip is about 65MB and the official documentation recommends using the ASYNC_UPLOAD parameter https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/asynchronous-upload-using-html5-application-deployer

I inserted the parameter in the following way in the mta.yaml

.....
modules:
- name: PMShift_ui_deployer
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  properties:
    ASYNC_UPLOAD: true
  requires:
  - name: PMShift_html_repo_host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - PMShiftModule-content.zip
      name: PMShiftModule
      target-path: resources/

But I continue to have the same error.

Can anyone help me?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Why Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) schedule job a...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Have you already seen the following SAP Help Portal document:

How to use Evaluated Receipt Settlement

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How to send control recipe to CPI (Cloud Platform Int...
entry.content:
CPI doesn't have an RFC sender adapter and the SPRO configuration is explicit regarding destination of type "T", so the general answer would be no unless you step down the path of modification which should be avoided.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Joule error in S4HC public when accessing SAC: No access to service
entry.content:
Dear all,

I'm currently testing Joule in a Partner TDD S4HANA Cloud public system... and since the Analytical insights feature seems now generally available I wanted to try this out by using SAC as source, as well.

My SAC system is also TDD, so it has already Version: 2025.8.3 - I know that the regular SAC Q2 update will come on the weekend May 17/18. So a regular SAC tenant might not yet work as of today.

I could include the SAC system successfully into the formation like described here.

My user settings in S4HC are in English, and my question is posted in English as well, since this is currently a prerequisite.

All 3 systems: S4HC, Joule and SAC use the same Identity Authentication Service, and I myself am system owner in SAC, so I have full authorization. We have a Import model indexed via Just Ask, and questions & answers there work fine.

But when using Joule the following error message appears:

Based on this response, it looks like Joule "knows" that it should access the SAC tenant, but obviously the login does not work 😞

My question is: Why not? What is the trick?

My assumption is, the root cause is lying somehow in the login procedure... but when I check in SAC: System - Administration - App Integration, I can see:

- One managed OAUTH Client: JOULE_OAUTH_CLIENT_etc_etc...

- One Trusted Identity Provider: JOULE_IDP_etc_etc...

So I would assume there is a working server to server communication?

FYI: Login into our SAC via the IDP is done via the specific SAML Attribute: User ID, which is also stored in the IDP User ID (If I would store the e-mail address there, I could no longer log into SAC. However mail address is used as Fallback method - without positive effect for my issue). The S4HC User of course is not aware of this. There we have the e-mail address, we have the HR ID P0000xx and we have a CB9980000xx auto-generated User ID.

Is anybody in the community here facing the same problem?

Or who is already able to use Joule with SAC results... and how is your SAC login configured?

Thanks for any feedback.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Not able to run MDK application through run as local...
entry.content: And when you create your MDK application are you selected the BTP destination as the source in the wizard? Does it correctly retrieve the list of entities?

entry.title: Re: How to call ATC in custom code?

entry.title: Creating a document with change number though SAP ECTR Connector API - C#
entry.content:
I'm attempting to automate the creation of document records through the use of C# and the PLM_conn API. I have got the snippet below mostly working, but I'm getting an error message regarding a missing change number requirement. 

Any information or examples of how to supply the change number (DRAW-AENNR)  to the method to create the document would be much appreciated. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/plm/3363506424.html

 public static void createMaster(IPlmConnector conn)
        {
            // Prepare template part
            string templateName = "C:\\temp\\20250508-sep_za065108_000.par";
            PlmRequestPartImpl templatePart = new PlmRequestPartImpl(templateName);
            // Prepare DocDefinition
            
            DocDefinition doc = new DocDefinition();
            doc.WithoutGui = true; // with or without showing document creation gui
            doc.DocDType = "SEP";
            doc.DocPart = "000";
            doc.DocNumber = "ROB-TEST1";
            doc.DocVersion = "00";
            doc.DocDescription = "CreateDocCTest";
                   
           

            // Prepare loaded parts
            PlmRequestPartList loadedParts = new PlmRequestPartList();
            // A: Creation without DocDefinition
            //IPlmResponse response = conn.CreateMaster( loadedParts, null, null );
            // B: Creation with DocDefinition 
            //IPlmResponse response = conn.CreateMaster( loadedParts, &Doc, null );
            // C: Creation without DocDefinition, with Template
            //IPlmResponse response = conn.CreateMaster(loadedParts, null, templatePart);
            // 😧 Creation with DocDefinition, with Template
           IPlmResponse response = conn.CreateMaster(loadedParts, doc, templatePart);
            // Evaluate response
            //evaluatePlmResponse(response);

            
        }

entry.title: Re: Custom MDK code for barcode reading logic is not work...
entry.content: Assuming I am understanding correctly that the camera scan on the zebra succeeds but he parsing does not, you will want to debug your rule to see why it is failing on the zebra then.

entry.title: How to compare two key figure values and copy in Data Action
entry.content:
Hello,

Users will enter the data at input page for two key figures Z_SALES1, Z_SALES2 along with other dimensions selections. The key figure Z_SALES1 value always higher than Z_SALES2 key figure for that dimension member combination, but sometimes user will enter Z_SALES1 lower values.

So, I am looking for the solution if users enter Z_SALES1 lower value compared to Z_SALES2, I would like to copy from Z_SALES2 values to Z_SALES1.

I tried the below code at Data Action but getting error –  “ No viable alternative at input ‘DATA’

IF DATA([d/Measures] = "Z_SALES1", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%) < DATA([d/Measures] = "Z_SALES2", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%) THEN

DATA([d/Measures] = "Z_SALES1", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%) =

RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Budget", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%,  [d/Measures] = "Z_SALES2")

ENDIF

How to fix the data action. How to achieve the required solution? Is the above problem statement, is it any other way to update except data action?

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: Re: How to find relevant batchjobs using variant input pa...
entry.content:
Hi @ali-sd ,

Please check the table TBTCP and/or logic of the report RSPC_DISPLAY_JOBS.

I could copy this report and rework accordingly to your needs. 

Or maybe develop a report to search for variants, using RS_VARIANT_CONTENTS or RS_VARIANT_VALUES_TECH_DATA and then apply for selection from TBTCP.

Hope this help.

BR, Dima

entry.title: Direct URL to open PO in SAP
entry.content:
Direct URL to open PO in SAP

We need to have a URL to display the PO number in ME23N by passing the PO number in URL.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Direct URL to open PO in SAP
entry.content:
Dear customer, 

Please, note that if the target mapping is well configured, you should be able to access the 

https://<yourURL>/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/shells/abap/FioriLaunchpad.html?saml2=disabled&sap-client=<yourClient>&sap-language=EN#PurchaseOrder-display?PurchaseOrder=<PONumber>

About how to maintain the right target mapping, please, check the following KBA: 

2766841 - Wrong document is displayed in the 'Display Purchase Order/Requisition/Contract - Advanced' app - SAP S/4HANA

In particular, please, make sure you have access to a target mapping PurchaseOrder-display configured with Transaction: MMPURPAMEPO and with parameter PurchaseOrder and target name P__EBELN. 

Anyway, in the KBA you have the transaction and parameters that you need to set. 

 

 Thanks and regards, 

 

Maria

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: Difference in BPEG and BPGE tables in sap PS?
entry.content:
The main difference between the BPGE and BPEG tables in the context of budgeting in SAP lies in the level of detail of the information stored:

  • BPGE: Stores the overall budget, meaning the total planned amounts for controlling objects (such as WBS elements or orders). It acts as a summary or aggregator of the budget.
  • BPEG: Stores the detailed (line-item) released budget entries, meaning each individual posting that makes up the total amount recorded in BPGE. This allows you to track every single budget line entered for the project.

In summary: BPGE shows the consolidated total, while BPEG provides the details of each individual budget entry that makes up that total.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to use "Run in parallel" within a S...
entry.content:
Hi @johnmurray,

I was facing the same issue but the strange part was out of 10 Sub Process's Input variables, only 2 variables were coming in the error and these 2 variables were marked as mandatory.

It worked for me by making the Input variables of the Sub Process as Non-mandatory.

Please try if that is the case for you as well.
Thanks & Regards,
Vishal Sharma

entry.title: Re: New Sandbox Available on the SAP Learning Site to Practice Hands-on
entry.content: Would I be able to create test users? @JenniferHauck

entry.title: Re: How do setup IAS login page for Onboardee and SSO pag...
entry.content:
If this is a strict requirement, and you can't find an alternative, what I would suggest is:

  • Create a separate subaccount for internal users.
  • Setup IAS to authenticate with Corporate IDP by default for this subaccount.
  • Deploy a simple Fiori/CAP application which redirects user to the SuccessFactors URL.

Internal users should then access the system through this link, instead of using the direct SuccessFactors URL.

IAS will automatically trigger SSO with Corporate IDP and they will be redirected to the SF URL. After this point, SF will redirect authentication request to the IAS and since the user is already authenticated, it will redirect back to the SF with successful login.

Let me know if the method is not clear on your side.

It is a dirty solution but if you don't have an alternative and this is something needs to be implemented, that would be an alternative.

entry.title: Re: Error when using "Run in parallel" within a SAP Build...
entry.content:
Hi Aqua ,

I was facing the same issue but the strange part was out of 10 Sub Process's Input variables, only 2 variables were coming in the error and these 2 variables were marked as mandatory.

It worked for me by making the Input variables of the Sub Process as Non-mandatory.

Please try if that is the case for you as well.
Thanks & Regards,
Vishal Sharma

entry.title: /DMO/FLIGHT maintain data public cloud system
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to generate data in /DMO/FLIGHT. there is a report SAPBC_DATA_GENERATOR (SE38). but I'm using public cloud system. public cloud we don't have GUI access. is there any way to add the data into sflight table?

Thanks,

Nirojan.

entry.title: Re: Data Initialization Issue in CAP Java Project with HANA Database on Deployment
entry.content:
Update:

  • I tried adding empty .csv files and deleted all the data from the tables.
  • After adding a new field to a table and deploying, the data was cleared as expected.
  • I then reinserted data into the tables, deleted the .csv files, and redeployed. This time, the data was not cleared.
  • However, when I modified a table again and redeployed, the data was cleared.

    In the deployer logs, I found the following message:

    Inserted 0 records of the data source file "myCsv.csv" into the target table "MY_TABLE"

    It seems like the .csv file is being cached or retained somewhere, even though it has been deleted from the project

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - IMAP and SMTP connection to Gmail with OAuth2 Authorization Code
entry.content:
@LiewJunYan thanks a lot for this excellent post. I was about to get mad while trying to make this work on my own today.

Extra frustration was added by the fact that the Token Servive URL given in the Client Secret Json was not the correct one for usage CPI.

entry.title: Currently investigating on LMS API basic Integration using OAuth Token server
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am new to LMS API's and investigating how to do the basic authenication from LMS to BiZX using OAuth Token Server access. My question is, the authenication is established by middleware integration like CPI or Boomi? I can I facilitate the pre-requisties to be performed in the LMS system?

I had a standard KBA from SAP but is shows how to integrate the web services and calls for Learning events. Can i have a basic informtaion in the child stage regarding the authenication?

Thanks for your support.

Many Regards

Moulika

SAP SuccessFactors Learning API

entry.title: Re: How to implement Batch Processing in SAPUI5 and Gateway Builder for oData V2?
entry.content: thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Web Client Extension Not Showing
entry.content:
Hi,
If the issue is still not resolved can you share the extensions log file from this /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/webclient/ location

entry.title: Re: Extract ECC
entry.content: ODBC connection to the database

entry.title: Re: Mass Creattion of Sub-WBS element
entry.content:
Yes, there is!
It is possible to perform mass creation of projects and/or WBS elements using tcode CNMASSCREATE.

The first step is to access the tcode and download the template by clicking the "Download CREATE Template" button. The file has four tabs.

In your case, since you only want to create WBS elements, fill in only the second tab of the spreadsheet, which refers to WBS elements, and leave the first tab, related to project definitions, blank.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Re: Tabela-Campo Chave de Acesso NF-e Saída / Table-Field...
entry.content:
Bom dia.

Pessoal, consegui, precisa concatenar os campos da tabela J_1BNFE_ACTIVE, conforme tabela abaixo, os quais geram a chave de acesso.

Obrigado.

=====================================================

Good morning.

I managed to do it, you need to concatenate the fields of the J_1BNFE_ACTIVE table, as shown in the table below, which generate the access key.

Thank you.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  
REGIORegion of Document IssuerJ_1BREGIO CHAR 20 
NFYEARYear of Document DateJ_1BYEAR CHAR 20 
NFMONTHMonth of Document DateJ_1BMONTH CHAR 20 
STCD1CNPJ Number of Document IssuerJ_1BSTCD1 CHAR 140 
MODELModel of Nota FiscalJ_1BMODEL NUMC 20 
SERIESeriesJ_1BSERIES CHAR 30 
NFNUM9Nine-Digit Document NumberJ_1BNFNUM9 CHAR 90 
DOCNUM9Random Number in Access KeyJ_1BDOCNUM9 CHAR 90 
CDVCheck Digit in Access KeyJ_1BCHECKDIGIT CHAR 10

entry.title: Re: A view on T012K showing different results than actual table?
entry.content:

Same issue I am facing...

In tcode se16h for Table t012k, 80 entries displayed but

In tcode se16h for CDS view on t012k, only 4 entries displayed and those are not related to actual client also..

 

 

Can you please help me on this

 

Thanks & Regards,

Harsh Ariwala

entry.title: Web Client Extension Error 404 - React App with React Router
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm trying to add tile extension to SAP B1 WebClient written in React.JS and it worked unless I added React Router functionality to it.

Now main page displays an error of 404. It is the problem with the address of a page, but I can't find a solution how to solve that. I added the 'homepage' tag as '.' to package.json like docs says but it didn't help.

If anyone knows the answer, please let me know. Thanks.

entry.title: Master Data Integration Internal Server Error with Instance Creation Failed
entry.content:
Hi
When I try to create an instance on BTP subaccount with plan, sap-integration I get Creation Failed with Internal Server Error.
It is for to sync with Subscription Billing.

I can create an instance on other subaccounts within the same Global account.

JSON is:

{
    "businessSystemId": "hrc-sub-bill-utilities",
    "enableTenantDeletion": true,
    "application": "hrc",
    "writePermissions": [{
            "entityType": "sap.odm.businesspartner.BusinessPartner"
        }, {
            "entityType": "sap.odm.product.Product"
        }, {
            "entityType": "sap.odm.finance.ExchangeRate"
        }
    ]
}

Any ideas? Appreciate any help.

Ger

entry.title: Monitor material coverage app not showing requirements as shortage
entry.content:
Dependent requirements are not showing in Monitor material shortage app under respective Shortage definition. Any Reason?

Here its mentioned that dependent reservation will show under stock day supply. OSDM1

Demand Profiles - OSDM0

MD04 screen

Monitor material coverage app shows nothing

But manage material coverage app shows the data

I am expecting this requriements should be shown as shortage in Monitor material coverage app. Is my understanding wrong?

entry.title: SAPPhire Orlando 2025: How agentic AI revolutionizes the way SAP is running support
entry.content:
My Session: How agentic AI revolutionizes the way SAP is running support, May 20, 2025 11:30 AM-11:50 AM EDT Session ID DIS1727

What you will learn about: In this session we will showcase how we’ve designed our first code based agentic scenario for customer support. We will showcase the business problem, the use case design as well as a short demo.

Looking forward to seeing you: I’m looking forward to seeing you to share with you our thoughts on a flexible and scalable architecture, a demo of the scenario as well as sharing lessons learned so far.

Now let’s hear from you - please feel free to share your thoughts/expectations/insights in the comments! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for comprehensive guidance, managed to complete custom task config.

What are the possibilities to further this as our custom task is manager to define needed equipments like phone and laptop, after manager completes this task, can we define ONB to sent email notification to helpdesk and/or report these afterwards?

Nina

entry.title: Re: Updation of CDHDR table
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Usage decision is not possible as Purchase order is a...
entry.content:
Hello @AV1976 

I don't think there is a good solution in your situation. I wonder how you managed to get yourself into such a trouble in the first place? How was it possible to archive and delete purchase orders with pending usage decisions? That looks like a huge gap that needs to be closed.

The MM_EKKO archiving object doesn't provide a reload program, at least not in standard. So you can't reload the missing purchase orders. 

The message M7150 comes from several places in the VBWS_CORRECTION_EKBE function module, which in turn is called from the VBWS_BATCH_VALUATION_CHANGE_UD function:

which in turn is called from the QMSP_VALUATE_CHARACTERISTIC function:

As far as I understand, the functions update batch classification during usage decision posting. You might try to clear e_post_classification under debugger, which would skip the update and error message. That won't update batch classification, but might allow to post the usage decision. Then you would need to verify batch classification. Those are just lose suggestions, not a ready-made solution. You need to test that thoroughly and verify all side effects. 

I'd also suggest contacting SAP support. They might have a better idea.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: /DMO/FLIGHT maintain data public cloud system
entry.content: SAPBC_DATA_GENERATOR is for the old table SFLIGHT, not for the recent /DMO/FLIGHT. Run the class /dmo/cl_flight_data_generator.

entry.title: SAP VIM Available services/API: OTBCINB/PF01_REGIST_DOC - API for document registration
entry.content:
Hi,

I have found the information bellow:

API for document registration
Starting with version 16.3.2, Business Center provides an API for the registration of
documents. This allows the start of Business Center processes from none-SAP
systems.
Available services:
• /OTBCINB/PF01_REGIST_DOC

 

Please, I have not found this API. 

Anyone knows how to access this API?

What kind of API is this? ODATA? Web Service(SOAMANGER) ?

 

Thanks for your help!!

 

Best Regards,

Delano.

entry.title: Re: Stop focus on control
entry.content:
Please follow

  • https://stackoverflow.com/a/78773169/5846045
  • https://stackoverflow.com/a/48559689/5846045

My above two answers should help. No need to use jQuery or dummy control.

entry.title: Visibility of Recruiting Applicants in Manage Pending Hires
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm currently implementing Recruiting integrated with Employee Central.

In the transformation template, we have the Target Role defined with R. The Recruiters don’t have permission to Manage Pending Hires (as per customer requirement).

In customer scenario, the users responsible for hiring the new employees (that have access to Manage Pending Hires) are not defined in Job Requisition, since they have no task in the recruitment process. These users have Manage Pending Hires permission, and the Recruiter is included in their target population, but for some reason, they are not able to see any candidates in Manage Pending Hires from Recruiting. 

 

Any idea on what might be missing?

Thanks in advance,
